title: Rhynchopsidium pumilum
taxonomy: Rhynchopsidium pumilum is classified within the plant family Crassulaceae, commonly known as the stonecrop family. This family comprises a wide range of plants, many of which are succulents or have succulent-like properties. Rhynchopsidium pumilum, like other members of the Crassulaceae family, is characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves that store water, making it well-suited to arid environments. The genus Rhynchopsidium is part of the tribe Kalanchoeae, which contains several other genera of flowering plants. The specific classification of Rhynchopsidium pumilum is subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.
